meaning: adj.无法辨认的,无法识别的,无法鉴定的
examples: Many of the bodies were unidentifiable except by dental records.
It's pretty much common sense: you want to buy food, not unidentifiable foodlike objects.
Henry Lockwood of Western New York is among them, unidentifiable in death and his burial location unknown until now.
Not only does that help avoid embarrassing gaffes by raising red flags for unidentifiable names, but it can turn any customer contact into a subtle sales tool.
